AgentMetadata.description:
Static OS metadata reported once at identity exchange. All fields optional —
agents that cannot determine a value omit it.
AgentMetadata.title: AgentMetadata

IdentityExchangeRequest.description:
Body of `POST /api/v1/agent/identity`.

`kind` identifies the signing mechanism (today: `aws-sts`).
`payload` is the JSON-encoded sigv4-signed STS GetCallerIdentity envelope.
`agent_metadata` carries static OS attributes (os, cpu_count, memory_bytes).
IdentityExchangeRequest.title: IdentityExchangeRequest

ReviewJob.description:
Read-side projection of a `review_jobs` row.

The SPA reads this via the `jobs_by_ticket` endpoint in
`reviewer/web.py`. The workflow-engine path doesn't emit this
shape — `workflow_executions` rows are the canonical job record.
ReviewJob.title: ReviewJob

Role.description:
Three-tier org role; Owner >= Admin >= Builder.

`OWNER` — full control incl. org deletion, billing, SSO config.
`ADMIN` — Owner powers minus deleting the org or removing other Owners.
`BUILDER` — read findings, post replies, trigger reviews, manage own acks.
Role.title: Role

SetVcsResponse.description:
Either the new state (when settings stored directly) OR an `install_url`
the SPA should navigate to (when the plugin requires an out-of-band install).
SetVcsResponse.title: SetVcsResponse

post.description:
Calls the plugin-supplied validator from the registry. Stamps
`last_validated_at` on success. Provider-specific HTTP lives in the
plugin, not here.

schema.title: Response Validate Key Api Api Keys  Provider  Validate Post
Response List Audit Api Audit Get

post.description:
Revoke every session for the user behind the current cookie. The
/account page's 'Sign out everywhere' button hits this.

get.description:
Return `{user, memberships}` for the cookie-bearer.

`memberships` lists the caller's current org memberships (slug, role,
handle, display_name). Revoked memberships disappear on the next call.
No `broken_integrations` — that field is served by
`GET /api/integrations/broken-summary` so integrations data stays in
the integrations module.

Lives on the public allowlist because the SPA hits it before any org
URL is selected. 401 when there's no session.

List registered provider ids. The SPA renders one button per id on
the login page; the test stub appears only when YAAOS_ENV=test.

schema.title: Response Providers Api Auth Providers Get

post.description:
Step-up endpoint: read the signed `yaaos_totp_challenge` cookie set
by the OAuth callback when the user needs MFA, verify the supplied
TOTP code, then mint the real session and redirect to the original
`next` path.

Mint a fresh (unverified) TOTP secret for the cookie-bearer. Returns
`{seed, otpauth_uri}`. The SPA renders the URI as a QR code; users on
devices without a camera type the seed. Verify must be called with a
current code before `verified_at` flips.

Verify a TOTP code against the user's enrolled secret. On success the
row's `verified_at` stamp flips and step-up login starts demanding a
code on every signin that wasn't satisfied by the IdP.

schema.title: Response Set Api Key Api Claude Code Api Key Post

get.description:
Code defaults for the orchestrator + sub-agents, plus the model /
version / effort dropdown enums. Imported at request time so a code
change to `defaults.py` surfaces on the next request — never cached.

schema.title: Response Defaults Endpoint Api Claude Code Defaults Get
Response Health Api Claude Code Health Get
Response List Endpoint Api Coding Agents Get
Response Uninstall Endpoint Api Coding Agents  Plugin Id  Delete
Response Health Api Github Health Get

post.description:
Owner-initiated GitHub App install. Returns the App's install URL with
a signed `state=<org_id>` query param. The SPA's button click POSTs here
(so `X-Org-Slug` + `X-CSRF-Token` reach the auth chain) and then sets
`window.location.href = redirect_url` to send the browser to GitHub.

The callback at `/install_callback` verifies the signed state and writes
a `github_app_installations` row.

get.description:
Post-install redirect target. GitHub redirects here with
`installation_id=<n>&state=<signed>` so we can bind the new install to
the right org.

Writes the binding directly into `github_app_installations` (the single
source of truth) with `account_login` fetched from GitHub's
`GET /app/installations/{id}`. Going through the App API rather than
waiting for the `installation.created` webhook means dev environments
without a webhook tunnel still get a complete install row.

Two-state response driving the Settings UI:
1. app_configured=False → platform GitHub App not provisioned (env vars blank).
2. installed=False → show "Install yaaos on GitHub" button.
3. installed=True → show "Manage on GitHub" + account info.

Live list of repos the App can see, fetched from GitHub via the
installation token. No yaaos-side allowlist — GitHub's install picker IS
the authority. Used by the Settings GitHub card's "Repositories" section.

Returns `{repositories: [{full_name, html_url, private}], total_count}`
when the App is installed; an empty list when it isn't.

schema.title: Response Repositories Api Github Repositories Get

get.description:
Return per-org broken-credential counts for the cookie-bearer.

Response: `{ orgs: [{ org_id, broken_integrations: [{ provider }] }] }`.
Only Owners + Admins receive non-empty `broken_integrations` lists; Builder-role
memberships always yield an empty list. 401 when there is no valid session.

list: q + repo multi + created_by + date range + sort.

All filters are AND'd. `sort` accepts `created_desc` / `created_asc`
/ `updated_desc`; anything else falls back to `created_desc` in
`LessonFilter`.

schema.title: Response Delete Lesson Api Lessons  Lesson Id  Delete

get.description:
Returns one row per registered provider so the UI can render the full
picker even before anything is connected. Unconnected providers come
back as `status="not_set"`.

schema.title: Response List Integrations Api Mcp Proxy Get
Response Clear Endpoint Api Mcp Proxy  Provider  Delete

get.description:
OAuth redirect target. Validates the signed state, exchanges the code,
persists the credential, redirects the operator back to the Integrations
settings page.

Mint a signed `state` carrying `(org_id, user_initiating)` and 303 to
the provider's authorize URL. The callback verifies the signature + uses
the embedded org_id (since the upstream doesn't know our X-Org-Slug).

schema.title: Response Validate Endpoint Api Mcp Proxy  Provider  Validate Post

post.description: The single proxy entry point. Bearer in `Authorization: Bearer ...`.

schema.title: Response List Members Api Memberships Get

post.description: Public-allowlist endpoint — session cookie identifies the acceptor.

patch.description:
Self-update one's `@handle` in the org named by the path param.
Enforces `UNIQUE(org_id, handle)` via the existing partial index —
duplicate handles surface as 409. The path `org_id` may differ from
`X-Org-Slug` (which the middleware still requires for the prefix gate).

schema.title: Response Remove Member Api Memberships  Target User Id  Delete

get.description:
Return the current org's top-level settings. Lets the SPA's Settings
page show what's actually set before the user edits.

patch.description:
Update top-level org settings. Body is a JSON object; only the keys
actually present are touched. Supports `session_timeout_override`
(null clears it, positive int sets minutes), `registered_iam_arn`, and
`aws_region`. A duplicate ARN already registered to another org returns
422 `arn_already_registered` — the app-layer check fires before the DB
write.

post.description:
create an org from the picker page (E2a.19).

The caller becomes Admin of the new org. Slug must be lowercase
a-z / 0-9 / hyphens and unique. Returns 409 `slug_taken` if the slug
is in use; 422 `invalid_slug` on bad characters; 401 on missing
session.

get.description: Aggregated readiness for the "not configured" gate.
Cross-org list of the user's memberships. Powers the org switcher and `/orgs` picker.

List workspace agents within the 1-hour UI-retention window for `slug`.

Visible to all org members (ORG_READ). Returns agents whose last heartbeat
is within the past hour; excludes older rows so the dashboard stays clean.

schema.title: Response List Org Agents Api Orgs  Slug  Agents Get

get.description:
Enumerate registered plugins of the requested type. The settings UI
consumes this for the VCS + Coding Agents pickers — no plugin id is
hardcoded in the frontend.

post.description: Cancel any non-terminal workflow_executions for this ticket.

: SPA "Ack" button. Marks the finding as `intentional` —
Builder sees this and accepts it for future reviews.

: SPA "Push back" button. Marks the finding as `wontfix` and

post.description:
Re-review a ticket — drives `pr_review_v1` via the workflow engine.

The SPA's only contract with this endpoint is the `scheduled_count`
field; the response carries `workflow_execution_id` so the caller can
poll workflow state if desired.

schema.title: Response Rereview Ticket Api Reviewer Rereview Post

get.description: Per-review timeline metadata.

schema.title: Response Reviews By Ticket Api Reviewer Reviews By Ticket  Ticket Id  Get

get.description:
Subscribe an SSE client to the general org-scoped event stream.

Returns `text/event-stream`; closes when the client disconnects. Each
frame is `data: <json>\n\n` carrying a `GeneralEventKind`-typed payload.
Only events published to the caller's resolved org reach this stream —
cross-org isolation is enforced by the per-org Redis channel shape.

Subscribe an SSE client to the per-workflow activity event stream.

Cross-org isolation is the channel key: subscribers attach to
`{caller_org}:workspace_activity:{wfx_id}`. A request for a wfx owned by a
different org subscribes to a channel nobody publishes to and yields an
empty stream.

schema.title: Response Get Org Sso Config Api Sso Config Get

put.description:
Upsert per-org SSO config. The exempt-Owner picker requires the
candidate to have a verified TOTP secret — otherwise reject with
`exempt_owner_no_totp`. Writes a `sso_config_changed` audit row + an
`exempt_owner_set` row when the exempt-Owner pointer changed.

schema.title: Response Upsert Org Sso Config Api Sso Config Put

get.description:
Find the SSO IdP (if any) matching the email's domain.

Drives the Login page's provider-button rendering:
`{provider: "github" | "saml", saml_org_slug?: str}`.

Looks up `sso_configs.email_domains` (JSONB array) for a row whose
`enabled = true` claims the address's domain. First match wins.
No SAML match → `{provider: "github"}` so GitHub-only orgs keep working.

`email` is required but only the format is validated — we never
confirm or deny that a given address belongs to a user.

schema.title: Response Sso Discover Api Sso Discover Get

post.description:
Assertion Consumer Service. Verifies the SAML response (real or stub),
matches the user by verified email, optionally JIT-creates a membership,
marks the session SSO-satisfied for this org.

get.description:
SP-initiated login. In POC test env we redirect to the test stub IdP
page (which the test then POSTs back via `/acs`). Production builds the
AuthnRequest XML via `plugins/saml`.
Public endpoint — operators hand this URL to the IdP.

List tickets per the contract.

Returns `{items, next_cursor}` instead of a bare array so the SPA can
drive Load-more pagination. `next_cursor` is null today — POC uses
naive limit pagination; opaque-cursor support lands when the result
sets grow.

Single-query Dashboard projection per E2a.3.

Response shape:
`{stats: {in_flight, hitl_pending, completed_today, failed_today},
   in_flight: [TicketRow ≤10],
   needs_attention: [TicketRow ≤5]}`.

Avoids the SPA making three `/api/tickets?status=…` calls in a tight
polling loop. `t.status` is the 5-state vocab post-collapse
(running / hitl / done / failed / cancelled); precise hitl/failed
counts depend on the workflow-state projection landing on every
transition.

schema.title: Response Dashboard Api Tickets Dashboard Get

get.description:
Per-ticket detail with the enrichment: `stages[]` (projected from
`workflow_executions`) + `builder` (the trigger identity).

Returns the Ticket pydantic fields plus:
- `stages: [{name, state, attempt_count, current_attempt, started_at,
   completed_at, workflow_execution_id}]` — one entry per workflow run
   on the ticket, newest first.
- `builder: {kind, user_id?, display_name, avatar_url?}` — `kind="user"`
   when the ticket's PR has an `author_login`; `kind="system"` when
   yaaos triggered the run with no human attribution.

schema.title: Response Detail Api Tickets  Ticket Id  Get

get.description: Aggregated timeline: ticket events + PR events for the ticket's PR.

schema.title: Response Audit Api Tickets  Ticket Id  Audit Get

get.description:
List past HITL exchanges (prompt + response + timestamps) for the
ticket per E2a.4 HITL tab "History" subsection.

Joins `pending_human_decisions` against the ticket's
`workflow_executions` rows. Newest exchange first.

schema.title: Response Hitl History Api Tickets  Ticket Id  Hitl History Get

post.description:
Submit a HITL response. Resolves the open `PendingHumanDecisionRow`
for the ticket's most recent awaiting-human workflow execution and
re-enqueues the routing step via `core.workflow.resume_hitl`.

Request body: opaque dict — passes through to the workflow engine's
`resume_hitl(response=...)`. The SPA's HITL renderer shapes this
per the prompt's discriminated-union schema (E2a.4).

Returns `{stage, next_state}` where `next_state` is the workflow
state immediately after the resume.

schema.title: Response Hitl Respond Api Tickets  Ticket Id  Hitl Respond Post
Response List Emails Api User Emails Get
Response Remove Email Api User Emails  Email Id  Delete

get.description: Profile payload for the User > Details page.

patch.description:
Update profile fields. `display_name` accepted as plain text; the
`github_username` denorm is owned by the login flow and is never written
here. `clear_github_username=true` removes the verified value.

delete.description:
Graceful-shutdown "going away" signal.

The agent sends this as the last action of its SIGTERM/SIGINT handler,
after stopping its heartbeat + claim loops and draining the WS. The
control plane eagerly:

1. Sets `workspace_agents.state=offline` + `last_shutdown_at=now`.
2. Revokes the bearer so subsequent calls 401 immediately.
3. Expires any workspaces owned by this agent and synthesizes terminal
   failure events for in-flight commands so their WorkflowExecutions resume.
4. Publishes `agent_liveness_changed` SSE so the dashboard flips the card
   offline without waiting for the sweeper's next tick.

Returns 204. Idempotent — calling on an already-offline/revoked agent is
harmless (bearer verify fails → 401 before this handler runs).

post.description:
Vault AWS-auth pattern: agent supplies a sigv4-signed STS
GetCallerIdentity in `payload`; control plane replays it against AWS,
canonicalizes the returned ARN, matches against `orgs.registered_iam_arn`,
checks the signed URL's region against `orgs.aws_region`, derives
`instance_id` from the role-session-name, persists/updates a
`workspace_agents` row keyed on `(org_id, instance_id)`, and issues a
1-hour bearer via `core.agent_gateway.bearers`.

The `X-Yaaos-Audience` header embedded in the sigv4 envelope must be
present and match `YAAOS_PUBLIC_HOSTNAME`. This binds the signed
request to a specific backend deployment and prevents replay against a
different yaaos instance.

Rotation: calling this endpoint again with a valid payload issues a new
bearer without revoking the old one. The old bearer remains valid until
its own `expires_at`. The agent atomically swaps the bearer in its HTTP
client after the rotation response arrives.

Failure modes:
- empty `payload` → 401 `unauthorized`, no audit row
- unsupported `kind` → 401 `unauthorized`
- audience mismatch → 401 `audience_mismatch`
- verifier failure (parse / shape / endpoint / body / replay /
  aws-rejected / clock-skew) → 401, structlog warning categorized
  by `FailureCategory`
- canonical ARN doesn't match any registered org → 403
  `forbidden_unregistered_arn`
- signed URL's region != `orgs.aws_region` → 401
  `sts_verification_failed` with category `region_mismatch`

schema.title: Response Get Connection Status Api Workspaces Connection Status Get
